LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SEAN MULVANEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE: PUNITIVE DAMAGES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE MOTION IS MOOT BECAUSE IT IS DIRECTED AT THE COMPLAINT, WHICH IS NO LONGER THE OPERATIVE PLEADING. (STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND V. SUPERIOR COURT (2010) 184 CAL. APP. 4TH 1124, 1131). MOREOVER, DEFENDANT FAILED TO SATISFY HIS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION OF PRESENTING SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFFS WILL BE UNABLE TO PREVAIL ON THEIR REQUEST FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. FURTHER, EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD SATISFIED ITS BURDEN, PLAINTIFFS PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE REGARDING THEIR ENTITLEMENT TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-06-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT SEAN MULVANEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION RE: PUNITIVE DAMAGES.
Appearances: Alex Merchant of the Tobener Law Center (504.2165) for plaintiff and responding party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. The motion is moot because it is directed at the complaint, which is no longer the operative pleading. (State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 1124, 1131). Moreover, defendant failed to satisfy his burden of production of presenting sufficient facts to show that plaintiffs will be unable to prevail on their request for punitive damages. Further, even if defendant had satisfied its burden, plaintiffs presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue regarding their entitlement to punitive damages. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

Ntc Of Mtn For Leave To File First Amended Complaint To Add New Deft Emily Hebb
Off Calendar Dec-06-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. TOM H. ELKE OF TOBENER LAW CENTER, TEL. NO. 504-2165 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS JEREMY MANN AND CARL DOBSKY'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint To Add New Defendant Emily Hebb IS Granted. No opposition filed. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =302/HEK
